message:
test(core): eliminate race on FSQLITE_RECURSIVE_CTE_DIRECT_SUM_CONSUMER_HITS

Global AtomicU64 counter was raced by parallel tests: one thread's
`store(0)` → query → `load()==0` sequence could interleave with another
thread legitimately firing the fast path and incrementing the counter,
producing non-deterministic pass/fail on `test_recursive_cte_direct_sum_consumer_declines_*`.

Fix: thread-local `Cell<u64>` counter wrapped in an `AtomicU64`-shaped
zero-sized handle (`RecursiveCteDirectSumConsumerHits`) that keeps all
call sites (production instrumentation + unit tests) call-compatible
with the previous API (`fetch_add`, `store`, `load`). Production
behaviour unchanged — the counter is observability-only and never
read by non-test code.

All 8 `test_recursive_cte_direct_sum_consumer_*` tests pass deterministically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
